raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, I made two purchases on XXXX using my American Express credit card. Also, on XX/XX/XXXX, I cancelled the purchase made on XX/XX/XXXX, and received from the merchant ( XXXX ) a refund. Due to currency fluctuations the amount differed slightly from the initial purchase amount. I kept the flight purchased on XX/XX/XXXX, until I received notification from the airline of a schedule change on XX/XX/year>, whereupon the change no longer worked for me and I decided to cancel the flight purchased on XX/XX/XXXX. All documentation received from the merchant indicated a refund would be sent back to my original payment method within 7 days. After 7 days elapsed, and no refund had been received, I opened a dispute with American Express on XXXX for the flight purchased on XX/XX/XXXX. However, on XX/XX/year>, I received notice that my dispute was closed because my account had been credited. This is an incorrect conflation of the cancelled XX/XX/XXXX purchase that had already been refunded ( ironically the refund amount was the same as the purchase amount for the XX/XX/XXXX flight purchase, again, due largely in part to currency conversion fluctuations ).
